diseases.overview: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted to people through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
diseases.definition: An acute febrile illness caused by Plasmodium parasites.
diseases.causes_or_pathogen: Plasmodium falciparum, Plasmodium vivax, Plasmodium ovale, Plasmodium malariae.
diseases.transmission: Vector-borne (Anopheles mosquito bites between dusk and dawn).
risk_factors: Living in or traveling to endemic areas (Africa, Southeast Asia, South America)
when_to_seek_medical_help: High fever after traveling to an endemic area
important_notes: Malaria is a medical emergency if not treated promptly. Falciparum is the most deadly strain.

diseases.overview: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral infection that causes a severe flu-like illness and sometimes a potentially lethal complication called severe dengue.
diseases.definition: A viral infection transmitted to humans through the bite of infected Aedes mosquitoes.
diseases.transmission: Vector-borne (Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus mosquitoes biting during the day).
risk_factors: Living or traveling to tropical/subtropical regions
Prior infection with a different DENV serotype
diagnosis: Blood tests detecting DENV, NS1 antigen, or antibodies
treatment: No specific antiviral treatment. Supportive care (hydration, pain relief). Avoid NSAIDs (aspirin, ibuprofen) due to bleeding risk.
when_to_seek_medical_help: Warning signs manifest 3-7 days after symptoms start: severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, bleeding gums, lethargy
important_notes: Do not take Aspirin or Ibuprofen, as they increase bleeding risk. Seek immediate help if warning signs appear after fever drops.

diseases.overview: Type 2 diabetes is a chronic condition that affects the way the body processes blood sugar (glucose).
diseases.definition: A metabolic disorder characterized by high blood sugar, insulin resistance, and relative lack of insulin.
diseases.causes_or_pathogen: Insulin resistance primarily caused by a combination of genetics and lifestyle factors.
severe_or_red_flag_symptoms: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (extreme confusion, profound dehydration)
when_to_seek_medical_help: Symptoms of diabetic complications (numbness, vision changes)
important_notes: Type 2 Diabetes can often be managed or even reversed early on with significant lifestyle changes.

diseases.overview: Hypertension is a condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high.
diseases.definition: Consistently elevated blood pressure in the arteries (>= 130/80 mmHg in US guidelines, >= 140/90 WHO).
diseases.causes_or_pathogen: Primary (essential): no identifiable cause. Secondary: underlying conditions (kidney disease, sleep apnea).
common_symptoms: Most people have no symptoms ('silent killer')
severe_or_red_flag_symptoms: Pounding in chest, neck, or ears
diagnosis: Blood pressure measurement using a sphygmomanometer
treatment: Lifestyle modifications (DASH diet, exercise, weight loss)
Medications (Diuretics, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, Calcium channel blockers)
prevention: Eat a healthy diet with less salt
when_to_seek_medical_help: Any red flag symptoms accompanied by high blood pressure
important_notes: Because it's often asymptomatic, regular screening is crucial for adults.

diseases.overview: Tuberculosis is a potentially serious infectious disease that mainly affects the lungs.
diseases.definition: An infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
diseases.transmission: Airborne (spread when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or speaks).
risk_factors: Living or traveling in areas with high TB rates
common_symptoms: Cough lasting three or more weeks
severe_or_red_flag_symptoms: Coughing up large amounts of blood
treatment: Long-term antibiotic therapy (6-9 months) typically using a combination of Isoniazid, Rifampin, Ethambutol, and Pyrazinamide.
home_care: Strict adherence to medication schedule is critical
important_notes: Stopping treatment early can cause drug-resistant TB, which is much harder to treat.
